content_markdown: Under which conditions of pressure and density will a real gas approximate to an ideal gas?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing four combinations of pressure and density labeled A through D.

vibe_explanation: A real gas behaves most like an ideal gas when the molecules are far apart and moving fast, such that intermolecular forces and the volume of the molecules themselves become negligible. This occurs at low pressure (molecules are spread out) and low density (fewer molecules per unit volume).
